mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering risk-off positioning that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month moving averages, with a hit rate below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which supports near-term equity momentum and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during tightening cycles, with a directional accuracy below 50% over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a low federal funds rate creates negative real yields and forces expectations of aggressive future tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% YoY and FEDFUNDS remains below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a strengthening labor market that supports household income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward returns across all market regimes, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in real gross domestic product confirms an economic recession, triggering corporate earnings downgrades and risk aversion that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 50% of quarters where GDPC1 registers negative quarter-over-quarter growth.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and total employment signal synchronized supply-side and labor market expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint indicator fails to predict positive 5-day returns when both series rise simultaneously, with a directional accuracy below 48% over rolling 6-month windows.
